title: The Org Chart Math Behind AI-Native Speed
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-03-11, Reading time: 2 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Ftomtunguz.com%2Fcommunication-tax-small-orgs%2F%3Futm_source=tldrfounders/1/0100019cdccc2fce-e1a2fbf0-abb8-4011-be17-54c075419121-000000/DltCbP-ne3tW1PZVj3YT-OcKvtju4UfKdt66pqevW6U=448
summary: The Org Chart Math Behind AI-Native Speed (2 minute read) Metcalfe's Law states that each new team member adds n-1 new connections. This means there are 11,175 potential communication channels in a traditional 150-person organization. An AI-enabled team producing equivalent output might need 30 people, dropping potential communication channels down to 435, a 96% reduction. AI-native startups are pulling ahead because fewer humans means fewer channels and faster iteration, compounding speed.

title: Building High Throughput Payment Account Processing
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-03-12, Reading time: 9 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fwww.uber.com%2Fblog%2Fhigh-throughput-processing%2F%3Futm_source=tldrdata/1/0100019ce1858e65-ac924faf-f872-4e88-9123-d2f6ad06af22-000000/Uyl8fkLy3lDU_UJ1MZlil80YzPSbCfwSpTfIZqzH5wU=448
summary: Building High Throughput Payment Account Processing (9 minute read) To handle extreme "hot-key" traffic on high-activity payment accounts which previously hit sequential processing limits of 3–4 ops/sec per account, causing multi-hour processing times for bulk jobs, Uber uses a three-service architecture to batch financial updates into ~250 ms windows (using Redis for coordination/queuing) with an atomic write per batch, and offloads immutable audit logs asynchronously.

title: Your Pipeline Succeeded. Your Data Didn't
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-03-12, Reading time: 12 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Frobertsahlin.substack.com%2Fp%2F2a4eac5c-06c7-4255-9be5-6989cc46e4b1%3Futm_source=tldrdata/1/0100019ce1858e65-ac924faf-f872-4e88-9123-d2f6ad06af22-000000/bTtEpEtsuAW9DybqeCYcyohjpJLzFlkGDARybIXCnJI=448
summary: Your Pipeline Succeeded. Your Data Didn't (12 minute read) A BigQuery-native anomaly detection system can catch silent data loss by monitoring ingestion volume across hundreds of tables using only built-in features like INFORMATION_SCHEMA logs and AI.DETECT_ANOMALIES. Implemented as a single dbt model, it detects abnormal drops in data volume without external tooling or per-table rules, helping teams identify partial pipeline failures that traditional pipeline success checks miss.

title: How I improved my analytics agent reliability from 45% to 86%
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-03-12, Reading time: 15 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fthenewaiorder.substack.com%2Fp%2Fhow-i-improved-my-analytics-agent%3Futm_source=tldrdata/1/0100019ce1858e65-ac924faf-f872-4e88-9123-d2f6ad06af22-000000/VxPc56tKA-3OcfggTHOdmQE3ZOLsB6hew5DGzkRrdXw=448
summary: How I improved my analytics agent reliability from 45% to 86% (15 minute read) Analyzing failures in an analytics agent and fixing issues in tests, date-selection rules, documentation, and especially the underlying data model improved reliability from 45% to 86%. The results show that most gains in context engineering come from clearer data models, explicit rules, and better documentation rather than complex agent architectures.

title: Announcing DuckDB 1.5.0
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-03-12, Reading time: 16 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fduckdb.org%2F2026%2F03%2F09%2Fannouncing-duckdb-150.html%3Futm_source=tldrdata/1/0100019ce1858e65-ac924faf-f872-4e88-9123-d2f6ad06af22-000000/Vz5AbmE26hwLomSbaK2rUlmKPOPA7fiSVN5MlnPOudI=448
summary: Announcing DuckDB 1.5.0 (16 minute read) DuckDB v1.5.0 introduces major advancements, including a reworked CLI, support for the VARIANT type (with binary storage for enhanced compression and query performance), and the elevation of GEOMETRY to a native core type with automatic column shredding—reducing storage by up to 3x. Notable concurrency and aggregate optimizations yield a 17% TPC-H SF100 throughput boost and up to 40% faster aggregates.

title: Scaling with Airflow 3.2: When to Defer and When to Use Native Async Operators
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-03-12, Reading time: 8 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Flinks.tldrnewsletter.com%2F107VHY/1/0100019ce1858e65-ac924faf-f872-4e88-9123-d2f6ad06af22-000000/CGTlN7NMgs9TIKVhxem53gcmmLEnueepIegMTC8Lw7s=448
summary: Scaling with Airflow 3.2: When to Defer and When to Use Native Async Operators (8 minute read) Apache Airflow 3.2 adds native async support to PythonOperator, allowing high-throughput I/O workloads to run concurrently inside a single worker without triggerer overhead. Combined with Dynamic Task Iteration, this approach dramatically improves performance for micro-batch tasks like API calls or SFTP transfers.

title: Your Docs Directory Is Doomed
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-03-12, Reading time: 5 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fyagmin.com%2Fblog%2Fyour-docs-directory-is-doomed%2F%3Futm_source=tldrdev/1/0100019ce1cbb249-3ab871ba-5657-4dc2-bdce-aa29ee513dd8-000000/iyj86V6YTqyq47D5Da31T6FK4bVRlvvcTw7zZVtbWOU=448
summary: Your Docs Directory Is Doomed (5 minute read) The common practice of managing context for LLMs through simple Markdown `/docs` directories is a failing, temporary solution. This approach leads to problems like discoverability issues, ownership ambiguity, "doc rot," and a lack of hierarchy. A “Context-Driven Development" (CDD) approach might be better, where context is strategically owned by the organization, and is made navigable, composable, and tightly linked to the codebase.

summary: Many SWE-bench-Passing PRs Would Not Be Merged into Main (15 minute read) A METR study found a gap between AI-generated code passing automated benchmarks and its acceptance by human maintainers. title: Chaos Engineering Practices to Increase Confidence and Reliability
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-03-13, Reading time: 5 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fengineering.monday.com%2Fchaos-engineering-practices-to-increase-confidence-and-reliability%2F%3Futm_source=tldrdev/1/0100019ce6f818bc-a3c366f1-c67a-4727-bba7-e17a32ca768d-000000/NpWPjm-sEmO_XX6eC5ZNOfkEjVpXWNXA7PJkPK-CWLk=448
summary: Chaos Engineering Practices to Increase Confidence and Reliability (5 minute read) Instead of hoping random failures expose weaknesses, the Monday team injects controlled failures in staging, such as network partitions, pod kills, and DNS outages. title: AI should help us produce better code
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-03-13, Reading time: 5 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fsimonwillison.net%2Fguides%2Fagentic-engineering-patterns%2Fbetter-code%2F%3Futm_source=tldrdev/1/0100019ce6f818bc-a3c366f1-c67a-4727-bba7-e17a32ca768d-000000/Xppu6qYF-yW7h2ZFmaGXpS9WSdAkOGUHT4Zj4Oy0avw=448
summary: AI should help us produce better code (5 minute read) While some developers fear AI will degrade code quality, coding agents can actually improve it by proactively addressing technical debt. Agents are great at handling time-consuming refactoring tasks, making it affordable to eliminate minor code smells and architectural inconveniences. Furthermore, AI tools allow developers to explore more options before finalizing designs.

title: The purpose of Continuous Integration is to fail
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-03-13, Reading time: 6 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fblog.nix-ci.com%2Fpost%2F2026-02-05_the-purpose-of-ci-is-to-fail%3Futm_source=tldrdev/1/0100019ce6f818bc-a3c366f1-c67a-4727-bba7-e17a32ca768d-000000/W45TL94rqDkbcWXPXl82o3YbiE10E6GXPdew9MYI05M=448
summary: The purpose of Continuous Integration is to fail (6 minute read) The true purpose and value of Continuous Integration (CI) is in its ability to fail, not to pass. CI acts as a safety net, identifying developer mistakes early in the feedback loop, which prevents faulty code from reaching deployment and incurring greater costs. Conversely, passing CI merely adds overhead without providing new value.

title: “Design Me a Highly Resilient Database”
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-03-13, Reading time: 9 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fnikogura.com%2FDatabaseDesign.html%3Futm_source=tldrdev/1/0100019ce6f818bc-a3c366f1-c67a-4727-bba7-e17a32ca768d-000000/VVA8R_D1ykX87kZW83LCVSxePp124nWZfip6SXs2jgU=448
summary: “Design Me a Highly Resilient Database” (9 minute read) Interview questions that ask candidates to "design a highly resilient database" without providing any context are pointless. For example, database resilience is entirely dependent on specific product requirements, such as data type, consistency needs (e.g., ACID for finance vs. eventual for IoT), and acceptable failure modes. Every database involves inherent tradeoffs.

title: Are LLMs not getting better?
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-03-13, Reading time: 3 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fentropicthoughts.com%2Fno-swe-bench-improvement%3Futm_source=tldrdev/1/0100019ce6f818bc-a3c366f1-c67a-4727-bba7-e17a32ca768d-000000/zolwte7fpPp5C_sgayfmN2wkvSUcv84O0otZSdonsuE=448
summary: Are LLMs not getting better? (3 minute read) LLMs aren't continuously improving their programming abilities necessarily. LLM performance has gotten worse under the stricter criterion compared to the typical benchmarks. Statistical analysis of merge rates since early 2025 suggests a constant or piecewise constant trend, rather than a linear upward slope, indicating a stagnation in capability for over a year.

title: Fintech's Moats Don't Compile

summary: Fintech's Moats Don't Compile (4 minute read) Fintech's primary moats reside in regulatory permissions, credit data, and risk-bearing capacity rather than replicable software code. While AI compresses margins for pure SaaS by commoditizing code, it strengthens fintechs that own their licenses and underwriting history. Competitive advantage is shifting away from UI toward those who control the "fin" side by managing money movement and proprietary risk models that AI optimizes but cannot replace.

title: Building Where the Buyers Are

title: Agentic Commerce
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-03-13, Reading time: 11 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Frobonomics.substack.com%2Fp%2Fagentic-commerce-202603%3Futm_source=tldrai/1/0100019ce759a51c-1768f909-1efc-48cd-8cae-b174603920ed-000000/qNnnsqOMWJBy1BotdXaWzypOjX5U7B0WCvx4WmizZ4o=448
summary: Agentic Commerce (11 minute read) Commerce has historically been closer to a seller's market. Consumers are largely shown what platforms choose to surface. AI agents will gradually shift the power toward the buyer. Instead of navigating the layer of market and platform incentives, AI will enable consumers to access the best product for their needs directly. This dynamic may be most powerful in B2B commerce, which has historically been opaque, fragmented, and relationship-driven.

title: Institutional AI vs Individual AI
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-03-13, Reading time: 16 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fwww.a16z.news%2Fp%2Finstitutional-ai-vs-individual-ai%3Futm_source=tldrai/1/0100019ce759a51c-1768f909-1efc-48cd-8cae-b174603920ed-000000/l07OBDABFgndIVuML5zqhzMoAWGu1Jyi_OxnQblXIdU=448
summary: Institutional AI vs Individual AI (16 minute read) Institutional AI could unlock far more value than individual AI by redesigning organizations alongside technology. Unlike individual AI, which boosts productivity with little firm value impact, institutional AI focuses on coordination, finding signals in data, countering bias, and scaling revenue. Successful organizations will be those that integrate AI as a foundational component, similar to the industrial shift to electric assembly lines.

summary: We built RLM for coding. And it F*cking rocks. Swarm native agents are here to stay (13 minute read) Slate is a frontier agent that uses a code environment for swarm orchestration. It can programmatically orchestrate and solve tasks by running a massive number of subagents. Slate supports a range of models and automatically selects the right model for the job. It uses novel context engineering and maximizes caching to lower costs.
